verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc quoque in amaritudine est sermo meus,  et manus plagæ meæ aggravata est super gemitum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־הַ֭יּוֹם מְרִ֣י שִׂחִ֑י יָ֝דִ֗י כָּבְדָ֥ה עַל־אַנְחָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mihi tribuat ut cognoscam et inveniam illum,  et veniam usque ad solium ejus ?
verses.king_james: Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ponam coram eo judicium,  et os meum replebo increpationibus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶעֶרְכָ֣ה לְפָנָ֣יו מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּ֝פִ֗י אֲמַלֵּ֥א תוֹכָחֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut sciam verba quæ mihi respondeat,  et intelligam quid loquatur mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֭דְעָה מִלִּ֣ים יַעֲנֵ֑נִי וְ֝אָבִ֗ינָה מַה־יֹּ֥אמַר לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolo multa fortitudine contendat mecum,  nec magnitudinis suæ mole me premat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבְּרׇב־כֹּ֭חַ יָרִ֣יב עִמָּדִ֑י לֹ֥א אַךְ־ה֝֗וּא יָשִׂ֥ם בִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Proponat æquitatem contra me,  et perveniat ad victoriam judicium meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁ֗ם יָ֭שָׁר נוֹכָ֣ח עִמּ֑וֹ וַאֲפַלְּטָ֥ה לָ֝נֶ֗צַח מִשֹּׁפְטִֽי׃
verses.king_james: There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ad orientem iero, non apparet ;  si ad occidentem, non intelligam eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן קֶ֣דֶם אֶהֱלֹ֣ךְ וְאֵינֶ֑נּוּ וְ֝אָח֗וֹר וְֽלֹא־אָבִ֥ין לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ad sinistram, quid agam ? non apprehendam eum ;  si me vertam ad dexteram, non videbo illum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂמֹ֣אול בַּעֲשֹׂת֣וֹ וְלֹא־אָ֑חַז יַעְטֹ֥ף יָ֝מִ֗ין וְלֹ֣א אֶרְאֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse vero scit viam meam,  et probavit me quasi aurum quod per ignem transit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־יָ֭דַע דֶּ֣רֶךְ עִמָּדִ֑י בְּ֝חָנַ֗נִי כַּזָּהָ֥ב אֵצֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vestigia ejus secutus est pes meus :  viam ejus custodivi, et non declinavi ex ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭אֲשֻׁרוֹ אָחֲזָ֣ה רַגְלִ֑י דַּרְכּ֖וֹ שָׁמַ֣רְתִּי וְלֹא־אָֽט׃
verses.king_james: My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.

verses.latin_vulgate: A mandatis labiorum ejus non recessi,  et in sinu meo abscondi verba oris ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִצְוַ֣ת שְׂ֭פָתָיו וְלֹ֣א אָמִ֑ישׁ מֵ֝חֻקִּ֗י צָפַ֥נְתִּי אִמְרֵי־פִֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse enim solus est, et nemo avertere potest cogitationem ejus :  et anima ejus quodcumque voluit, hoc fecit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְה֣וּא בְ֭אֶחָד וּמִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶ֑נּוּ וְנַפְשׁ֖וֹ אִוְּתָ֣ה וַיָּֽעַשׂ׃
verses.king_james: But he is in one mind, and who can turn him? and what his soul desireth, even that he doeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum expleverit in me voluntatem suam,  et alia multa similia præsto sunt ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֭י יַשְׁלִ֣ים חֻקִּ֑י וְכָהֵ֖נָּה רַבּ֣וֹת עִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et idcirco a facie ejus turbatus sum,  et considerans eum, timore sollicitor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֭ן מִפָּנָ֣יו אֶבָּהֵ֑ל אֶ֝תְבּוֹנֵ֗ן וְאֶפְחַ֥ד מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus mollivit cor meum,  et Omnipotens conturbavit me.
verses.king_james: For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim perii propter imminentes tenebras,  nec faciem meam operuit caligo.
verses.king_james: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
